column10: Overall F1 0.863 sits in the documented intermediate-C++ / weak-design-pattern band. The analyzer holds up against the cohort: half the decisions are right, the misses are concentrated on the patterns the cohort openly does not know.

High-familiarity pattern: participants recognised it accurately (TP=37) but also over-attributed it on unrelated lines (FP=1). Strong recall (0.97), moderate precision (0.97) — matches the "intermediate-C++ defaults to Singleton/Factory" tendency.

Low-familiarity pattern: only 18 TP(s) in the cohort, but those who picked it picked it right (recall 1.00). Precision 0.82 is the bottleneck — FP=4 hallucinations from unrelated lines outnumber the real picks. Tier-level reading is more honest than per-pattern F1 at this decision count.
